Because this area was former territory of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{jalink|Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{jalink|Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{jalink|Buddhist|Q748}} {{jalink|temple|Q5393308}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{jalink|Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773}}. It is said that {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} granted this land to {{jalink|Nichira|Q11510262}}, who had been called back from {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くすもとしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '木の本：大阪府八尾市木の本1-59 北木の本：大阪府八尾市北木の本5-73 南木の本：大阪府八尾市南木の本7-33', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府八尾市木の本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135009152|ltq=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == Commons category (P373) == * Kusumoto-jinja (Yao) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039150|ltq=Q135039150|WD=Q135039150|1=Makimotono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '木の本：大阪府八尾市木の本1-59 北木の本：大阪府八尾市北木の本5-73 南木の本：大阪府八尾市南木の本7-33', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府八尾市木の本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くすもとしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '木の本：大阪府八尾市木の本1-59 北木の本：大阪府八尾市北木の本5-73 南木の本：大阪府八尾市南木の本7-33', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府八尾市木の本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '木の本：大阪府八尾市木の本1-59 北木の本：大阪府八尾市北木の本5-73 南木の本：大阪府八尾市南木の本7-33', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府八尾市木の本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|WD=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|WD=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|WD=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|WD=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|WD=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|WD=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]]

It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická in Kawachi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 80 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039149|ltq=Q135039149|qid=Q135039149|1=Kurotano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Shikinagayoshi Shrine|lt=Shikinagayoshi database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Commons category (P373) == * Kusumoto-jinja (Yao) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039150|ltq=Q135039150|qid=Q135039150|1=Makimotono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '木の本：大阪府八尾市木の本1-59 北木の本：大阪府八尾市北木の本5-73 南木の本：大阪府八尾市南木の本7-33', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府八尾市木の本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:樟本神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11542244}} == Japanese content == '''Kusumoto Shrine''' (くすもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kusumoto Shrine, three seats, Kuwa-yuki, Shiki District, Kawachi Province," and today it is enshrined at three locations in Yao: Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Tanboku District|Q11368489|qid=Q11368489|1=Tanboku district}}), Kita-Kinomoto, and Minami-Kinomoto (former {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q11491125|qid=Q11491125|1=Shiki district}}). === Overview === Its founding date is unknown, but it is thought to date back to before the Kofun period. The enshrined deity was originally regarded as {{ill|lt=kodama|Q1515938|qid=Q1515938|1=Kodama (spirit)}} (tree spirit), and later identified with {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|qid=Q11059754|lt=Futsu Omikami|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} and {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was former territory of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, it is thought that deities revered by {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|qid=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} as guardian gods were brought here in the reign of Emperor Yomei. Originally there were three seats in Kimoto village, but when Kita-Kimoto split off and later Minami-Kimoto split off, one seat each was divided to those villages. === Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kinomoto) precincts |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kinomoto 1-59 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|4|N|135|35|14|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in the old Kimoto settlement of Kinomoto 1-chome. From the Minami-Kinomoto Kusumoto Shrine, go one intersection south and two intersections west. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Kita-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Kita-Kinomoto) worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kita-Kinomoto 5-73 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|qid=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), ike.jpg|Pond said to be where Moriya's head was washed File:Kusumoto-jinja (Kita-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Minami-Kinomoto === {{神社 |名称=Kusumoto Shrine |画像=[[File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|qid=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|qid=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|qid=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|qid=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image. File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), dou.jpg|Nichira-ji main hall File:Kusumoto-jinja (Minami-kinomoto, Yao), torii.jpg|Torii {{-}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kusumoto-jinja (Yao)}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kusumoto Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:大正 (八尾市)]]

Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|32|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the former Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line (former Yao-Fujiidera Line). In the precincts is a pond about 3 meters in diameter said to be where {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|qid=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}'s head was washed. There is a children's park in the center of the grounds, with a small shrine building at the back. Among the three shrines, this one is the smallest in scale. Kusumoto Shrine (Minami-Kinomoto) precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Minami-Kinomoto 7-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}} |祭神=Kodama (also called Futsu Omikami) |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) |例祭=Kinomoto: July 15 and October 9
Minami-Kinomoto: October 9-10 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kusumoto Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|36|5|N|135|35|21|E}}}} }} It is on the west side along the city road (former Yao-Fujiidera Line) a little south of the Kinomoto intersection on the old Osaka Chuo Kanjo Line. The shrine buildings are on the north side of the grounds. On the south side is '''Nichira-ji''', a {{ill|lt=Buddhist|Q748|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|qid=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the Obaku school; its principal image is {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|qid=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. It is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} granted this land to {{ill|lt=Nichira|Q11510262|qid=Q11510262|1=Nichira}}, who had been called back from {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|qid=Q28428|1=Paekche}}, and Nichira built a temple here and enshrined a Yakushi Nyorai image.
